items.title: Man found dead in fire at block of units in Ballarat's Wendouree

children.content: The man was pulled from the house by emergency crews, but could not be revived.

gallery.altText: brick unit with police officers and police tape

thumbnail.altText: brick unit with police officers and police tape

items.title: Sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms

children.content: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.

image.altText: A Victoria Police car is parked in front of a billboard with a Victoria Police uniform close up

items.title: 'Crisis situation': The Allan government is scrambling to remake donation laws

children.content: With only six months before the state election, the Victorian government struggles to devise new donation laws after the High Court found previous laws unconstitutional.

image.altText: Jacinta Allan appears serious, standing inside.

items.title: Melbourne nightclub has operated for two years without insurance

children.content: Pride of our Footscray stopped paying public liability insurance when 18 insurers refused to underwrite the venue. The manager says operating without cover keeps staff on tenterhooks.

gallery.altText: A drag performer in a large blonde wig, pink glitter eyeshadow, false lashes and pink lipstick in a pink dress.

thumbnail.altText: A drag performer in a large blonde wig, pink glitter eyeshadow, false lashes and pink lipstick in a pink dress.

items.title: Qantas plane diverted after passenger allegedly bites flight attendant

children.content: Fellow passengers are believed to have assisted after a flight attendant was allegedly bitten on plane travelling between Melbourne and the US, with the plane diverted to Tahiti.

image.altText: Two men face each other in a plane

items.title: Protesters march at Nakba Day rallies around Australia

children.content: Crowds gather at rallies around the country to commemorate the anniversary of the Nakba, a day that recognises the mass displacement of Palestinians in 1948.

image.altText: A agthering of people on a city street with some holding Palestinian flags.

items.title: Police reopen cold case into armed robbery at Melbourne shopping centre

children.content: Police are appealing for help to find the man believed to be behind a 1994 armed robbery at a Chadstone cinema that left three other men with gunshot wounds.

image.altText: An image released by Victoria Police showing the face of a man with blonde hair wearing a dark beanie.

items.title: From cask to car, could red wine be Australia's next fuel?

children.content: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.

image.altText: A posed photo of someone supposedly pouring wine into the petrol inlet of a white car.

items.title: How Pokémon cards are fuelling crime in Melbourne

children.content: Rare Pokémon cards can sell for hundreds or even thousands of dollars, with a record sale of one Pikachu card going for over $22 million earlier this year.

gallery.altText: Alex stands in front of a counter in a trading card shop store filled with stock

thumbnail.altText: Alex stands in front of a counter in a trading card shop store filled with stock

items.title: 'Destroys trust in the system': Rally held for Isla Bell

children.content: A rally has been held for Isla Bell in Melbourne after friends and family were left devastated when murder and manslaughter charges were dropped against the man accused of killing the 19-year-old.

image.altText: Hands holding a photo of Isla Bell

items.title: Scott Morrison joins historic bronze collection in avenue of honour

children.content: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

image.altText: Scott Morrison standing next to a bronze bust of himself.

items.title: Bushwalker takes photo of rare bug in the wild

children.content: There were no known photographs taken in the wild of the
Myrmeleon houstoni
until a regional Victorian woman snapped a photo of one while on a bushwalk.

gallery.altText: A woman crouched down in her garden taking a photo of a plant with her phone

thumbnail.altText: A woman crouched down in her garden taking a photo of a plant with her phone

items.title: Boy dead, two injured in car crash in north-west Victoria

children.content: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

image.altText: A car crashed against a tree on the side of a highway with debris strewn near the car.

items.title: 'Don't be a d**k, Dezi!': New claims about Freeman police shooting

children.content: The owner of the Porepunkah property where Dezi Freeman shot two police officers says he rang and spoke to Freeman soon after the shooting.

image.altText: A man stands against a rural property

items.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day

children.content: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

gallery.altText: A close up of a woman starring off to the left with a serious look on her face

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a woman starring off to the left with a serious look on her face

items.title: Big money and a free degree: Athletes choose US college football over AFL

children.content: A new generation of Australian athletes is skipping the MCG entirely, eyeing US college football scholarships instead of Aussie Rules.

image.altText: A red headed 19 year-old boy with a football on a farm.

items.title: Victorian ex-premier Andrews to be immortalised with $134k statue

children.content: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

image.altText: A portrait of Daniel Andrews smiling.

items.title: Shoplifting is so bad in this town that police are homing in

children.content: In the regional Victorian town of Shepparton, shoplifting and retail crime are so rife that police have launched a dedicated patrol unit to target repeat offenders.

image.altText: RETAIL THEFT HERO

items.title: Ali Moore to hang up headphones as 774 Melbourne Drive host

children.content: Veteran broadcaster Ali Moore will step away from the show at the end of the month, with Charlie Pickering to join the program as the new host.

image.altText: Ali Moore

items.title: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise

children.content: A drawn out pay dispute between Victoria's government and public sector teachers is set to come to an end, with the union reaching an in-principle deal for pay rises between 28 and 32 per cent.

gallery.altText: Teachers cheer during speeches at the strike rally in Melbourne.
Dozens of people sit around tables with one hand raised in the air.

thumbnail.altText: Teachers cheer during speeches at the strike rally in Melbourne.
Dozens of people sit around tables with one hand raised in the air.

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: Surge with handball or control by foot: AFL challengers make choices in 2026

children.content: An early review of the AFL season so far points to a split between two distinct styles, with one side making a case for being flag favourites by showing it can deliver both.

image.altText: A Hawthorn AFL player races up field

items.title: Police believe man found dead in Tarneit was kidnapped by armed intruders

children.content: A man's body was found near a children's playground in Melbourne's west after a violent home invasion in the city's north.

gallery.altText: Blue and white police tape surrounds a park which contains a children's playground on a sunny morning.

thumbnail.altText: Blue and white police tape surrounds a park which contains a children's playground on a sunny morning.

items.title: Carlton interim coach Fraser pulls out of running for full-time role

children.content: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

image.altText: Josh Fraser speaks to media wearing a Carlton cap

items.title: Cocaine hidden in imported fruit pulp worth estimated $4.6m, AFP says

children.content: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.

image.altText: Op Lonsdale 03

items.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch

children.content: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a vessel towing devices on the surface.

items.title: Firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc

children.content: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.

image.altText: road-side sign of planned burns being patrolled

items.title: 'Appalling' lack of response to suspected overdose rates at Crown, expert says

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, with the casino complex averaging an ambulance call-out for a suspected overdose every two weeks, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

gallery.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: Teenagers charged over arson attack on Melbourne CBD bar

children.content: Police allege Bar Bambi in ACDC Lane has again been targeted by arsonists, while officers also investigate suspicious truck fires at two businesses in Melbourne's inner-west.

image.altText: Police tape outside a bar.

items.title: Shed weight, go slow and pump up your tyres to save on fuel

children.content: How to reduce your petrol bill by changing your driving technique and tinkering with your car.

image.altText: A fuel nozzle in a car's fuel filler inlet.

items.title: Child sex offender resigns as Melbourne Karen Buddhist group president

children.content: A convicted child sex offender who was told he was exempt from requiring a Working With Children Check has resigned as the president of a community organisation.

gallery.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

avatar.alt: A headshot of Nate standing in front of a dimly lit building wearing a jacket.

items.title: As the eldest daughter, I always knew I would care for my parents

children.content: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

image.altText: Cindy Mai and her mum when she was 11  years old

items.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of 'Sammy' the seal

children.content: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

image.altText: A seal sleeps on a rocky wall, looking towards the camera

items.title: MPs join calls for mayor in Melbourne to resign over character reference saga

children.content: The mayor of Wyndham City Council, Preet Singh, has faced protests and calls to quit after he wrote a character reference for a man convicted of child sex offences.

image.altText: A man in mayoral ceremonial robes poses for a professional portrait.

items.title: Former top barrister spared jail after trying to overcharge clients

children.content: Norman O'Bryan was one of Melbourne's top barristers — until he was caught trying to overcharge his clients in a scandal described as "one of the darkest chapters" in Victorian legal history.

image.altText: A man in a suit leaves court
